Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-06-2020 Continued To Feb-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Cahn in Propria Persona.

Ms. Cahn stated that she filed an amended petition on December 23rd, but she could not provide a hard copy of the order, that the Clerks will not provide information/assistance, that she could not find the document (order) and that it is not on the Court's website. The Court explained that the legislature makes the rules, that all of the rules apply to everyone's estates, that it is a state rule (LRSF 14.93) to submit a proposed order. The Court advised Ms. Cahn to get a nolo press book. The Petition for Final Distribution is continued to February 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Trausch. Not reported.

Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-16-2019 Continued To Jan-06-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Jun-24-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Jun-10-2019 Continued To Jun-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Cahn in Propria Persona.

Ms. Cahn stated that she was told by the bank that they do not sign Court documents, that she went back to the bank and spoke with someone else, and that the bank now has the form. Ms. Cahn also stated that there is $175,000.00, that she is in the process of selling the car and that she will add those funds to the account. Status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account is continued to June 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Cahn in Propria Persona.

Ms. Cahn stated that she has no experience in probate, that she believes she needs letters of testamentary, that there is a checking account and a savings account that she is not allowed to access, and that she needs to sell the car. Ms. Cahn also stated that she contacted the creditors, that she paid all of the creditors out of her own funds, that there are no creditors, that no taxes are owed and that her Brother will be receiving a refund. In addition, Ms. Cahn stated that she understands that she can not take any of the money, that she is the named beneficiary and the administrator, and questions if she is required to do an inventory. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Ms. Cahn is ordered to place the estate funds into a blocked account in lieu of bond. Status on close of estate will be set by the Court. Ms. Cahn was informed that the order will indicate the name of the Probate Referee if an inventory and appraisal is required.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
